subject: Gaming
title: The FTC and Microsoft-Activision
description: A quick, practical guide to The FTC and Microsoft-Activision.

hook.text: The FTC tried to block Microsoft's $69 billion Activision purchase. It was gaming's biggest antitrust battle.

buildup.text: The Federal Trade Commission argued the deal would harm competition by giving Microsoft too much power.

discovery.text: Microsoft argued the deal was about mobile gaming and would help them compete with Sony, not dominate.

twist.text: The FTC lost in court. The judge ruled the deal was legal. It was a rare public defeat for the agency.

climax.text: The deal closed in 2023. Microsoft now owns Call of Duty, World of Warcraft, and Candy Crush.

punchline.text: The FTC lost. Sixty-nine billion approved. Gaming's biggest deal went through.

quiz.question: What was the outcome of the FTC's attempt to block the Microsoft-Activision deal?
options: The FTC lost in court and the deal was approved
